text: The angularity of my perspective unsettles me at first—it reminds me that my viewpoint is always a little off-kilter, never fully objective. The transparent glass divides the space, evoking both separation and possibility. I watch preparations unfold behind the glass—people setting up, perhaps for a discussion or a performance. I feel a mixture of anticipation and detachment, aware that I am both observer and participant in this unfolding scene.
